message:
test(bench): replace go_flag with request/completed counter pair in condvar bench

The bench_condvar_wait_signal_roundtrip benchmark used a single
AtomicU32 go_flag with a fixed iters_per_round loop that the
signaler thread spun on cv2.nwaiters > 0 and then issued a single
condvar_signal per iteration. Two failure modes:

1. The signaler can race past a wait-signal pair when the waiter
   wakes, releases the lock, and re-enters cv_wait before the
   signaler has noticed nwaiters dropping back to zero — the next
   loop iteration still sees nwaiters > 0 (set by the new wait)
   and fires a stray signal that produces a spurious wakeup.
2. The signaler had no way to distinguish "main thread wants me
   to do the next round" from "I'm running ahead" — the bench
   relied on the spin-on-nwaiters check holding, which under heavy
   contention can mis-order the request/response.

Replaced with a request_id / completed_id / done counter triplet:

- request_id: incremented by the main thread once per round to
  ask the signaler to issue a signal.
- completed_id: incremented by the signaler after each successful
  signal; the main thread waits for completed >= requested before
  the next round.
- done: terminator flag. The signaler exits when done != 0 and
  completed has caught up to requested.

The signaler's loop body is now:

    if done && completed >= requested { break; }
    if requested == 0 || completed >= requested { spin; continue; }
    if cv2.nwaiters == 0 { spin; continue; }
    condvar_signal(cv); completed += 1;

This is a benchmark-correctness fix only — no production code is
involved and the measured number is now what we always claimed it
was: time per request/response round-trip, not "iters_per_round
signals fired into the void".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
